items.summary: Claude Guillemot, who founded Ubisoft with his four brothers, has died at the age of 69.

items.title: Polymarket reportedly paid creators to post deceptive videos about fake bets
items.summary: Many of those videos were reportedly filmed on “near-perfect copies” of the Polymarket website, while featuring trades and winnings that were not real.

items.title: When the Trump administration cracks down on Anthropic, who benefits?
items.summary: On the new episode of Equity, we discussed what actually prompted the administration's latest moves against Anthropic, and what this might mean for the AI ecosystem.

items.title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
items.summary: Siri’s AI overhaul may have grabbed the headlines at WWDC, but some of Apple’s most useful AI features are arriving elsewhere in iOS 27.

items.title: Signal’s Meredith Whittaker wants you to remember that AI chatbots ‘are not your friends’
items.summary: "These are not your friends. These are not conscious beings. These are not sentient interlocutors.”

items.title: He made your free video player run smoothly. Now he’s doing that for robots.
items.summary: French serial entrepreneur and open-source legend Jean-Baptiste Kempf has been building Kyber, an infrastructure layer to control remote devices in real time.

items.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
items.summary: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

items.title: Go eyes robotaxis and acquisitions after Japan’s biggest IPO of 2026 — here’s why it matters
items.summary: Go's IPO — Japan's biggest so far this year — has done more than provide a much-needed boost to the country's languishing listing season. It has also supplied the taxi-hailing app with the capital required to address an existential issue: Japan's shortage of drivers.

items.title: Aura’s impressive e-ink photo frame doesn’t even look digital
items.summary: All of Aura's frames connect to the Aura app, which is where you can upload photos from your phone, web, email, iCloud, or Google Photos.

items.title: Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
items.summary: Fusion startups have raised $7.1 billion to date, with the majority of it going to a handful of companies.

items.title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
items.summary: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails.  Cybersecurity researchers have since signed an open letter calling the move dangerous, and Anthropic itself noted the same jailbreaks exist in other models. So is […]

items.title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China, but how?
